title: Question: Parameter in package used for signal width
body:
---


Author Name: **Julius Baxter** (@juliusbaxter)
Original Redmine Message: 918 from https://www.veripool.org



---

If I have a SystemVerilog package declaring a parameter, and then attempt to use that parameter to define the width of a signal, I get a syntax error.

My testcase, which uses 2 files, is as follows.

mypackage.sv:

 package MY_TYPES; 
    parameter a_width = 2;   
 endpackage // MY_TYPES

testcase.sv:

 module testcase(); 
    reg [MY_TYPES::a_width-1:0] testreg; 
 endmodule // testcase


And compiling with:

 verilator -sv --lint-only mypackage.sv testcase.sv

I get:

 %Error: testcase.sv:3: syntax error, unexpected '-', expecting '(' or '.'
 %Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)

However, other EDA tools have no problem with this usage of parameters in packages.
